fields.description:
## Why
The engine must execute workflows deterministically and survive crashes/concurrency on an
event-sourced, auto-pushing store, identically across library/CLI/MCP.

## What
- Thin linear executor (graphlib.static_order) running scripted + agent steps.
- Persist run-state as new WORKFLOW_RUN/WORKFLOW_STEP event types (per-key LWW map), read back
  via replay for the async status tools.
- Idempotency: WORKFLOW_STEP marker committed AFTER the effect; forward-only recovery;
  (run_id, step_id) handed to downstream APIs as their idempotency token.
- Async over MCP: run_workflow returns a run_id; add get_workflow_status / get_workflow_result.

## Scope
- In: executor, run-state events, idempotency, async MCP entrypoints, TTL orphan-tmpdir sweep.
- Out: agent-step internals + snapshot creation/teardown (WS-D); scripted steps (WS-E).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Linear executor; Burr-tripwire CI test scoped to executor.py ONLY (not the package) asserting none of asyncio/concurrent.futures/threading/multiprocessing/retry-libs is imported; carries the written Burr-adoption trigger list; executor state pre-shaped toward an immutable Burr-style State.
- [ ] WORKFLOW_RUN/WORKFLOW_STEP event types wired (KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES, EVENT_TYPES, per-key LWW processor, compaction preserve, replay read-back).
- [ ] Globally-unique run_id; marker-after-effect idempotency; forward-only recovery.
- [ ] now()/uuid4()/random()/live reads captured once and persisted in the WORKFLOW_STEP record for deterministic replay.
- [ ] WS-C owns the TTL orphan-tmpdir sweep (snapshot create + finally-teardown belong to WS-D).
- [ ] run_workflow (async, run_id) + get_workflow_status/result across lib/CLI/MCP.
